_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
procedure_name: Management of Nerve Plexus / Tendon injuries (Tendon injury repair)
clinical_indications: Inability to bend or extend a finger due to tendon injury
Underlying tendon injury in clean cuts requiring early repair for optimal results
Contaminated wounds with tendon injury requiring adequate wound debridement and re-assessment after 48 hours for further decision making
Gross tissue loss and exposed tendons requiring adequate wound debridement, early soft tissue cover (local/regional/free flaps), and delayed repair

preauth.label: Clinical Notes detailing the injury and need for surgery
Medico legal case report/ FIR copy if due to accident
Optional Nerve conduction velocity EMG report MRI report

ppd_preauth.q: Were the clinical notes suggestive of tendon injury?
Clinical notes – details of accident, signs & symptoms, indication for surgery, and planned line of treatment?

common_queries: Clinical notes not suggestive of tendon injury
Clinical notes lack details of accident, signs & symptoms, indication for surgery, or planned line of treatment
Medico legal report/FIR copy missing for accident-related injury
Relevant optional reports (nerve conduction velocity/EMG/MRI) not submitted when specifically indicated by clinical findings

clinical_key_pointers: Patient usually presents with c/o inability to bend or extend a finger
To be ruled out in cuts involving fingers, palm and dorsum of hand, wrist and forearm
Mechanism of injury to be noted
Look whether it's a clean or contaminated wound
Give thorough wound wash after administering adequate analgesia
FDS, FDP, FPL, FCR, FCU to be tested and zones of flexor tendon injury to be noted
APL, EPB, ECRL, ERCB, EPL, EI, EDC, EDM, EhCU to be tested and zones of extensor tendon injury to be noted
Vascularity of the affected finger/hand to be documented by capillary refill or pinprick
X-ray of the involved part to rule out fractures and foreign body
Associated vascular or nerve injury to be documented
surgical_options: Early repair in clean cuts with underlying tendon injury
Debridement and re-assessment in contaminated wounds
Debridement followed by early soft tissue cover (local/regional/free flaps) and delayed repair in gross tissue loss and exposed tendons
